title: US-3: Streaming Ollama endpoint + prompt assembly
description:
New API route that substitutes test values into placeholders and streams Ollama response.

## Details
- New endpoint: POST `/api/playground/run`
- Request: `{ prompt: string, model: string, ollamaUrl: string, systemPrompt?: string }`
- Client-side: substitute test values into placeholder positions before sending
- Server-side: call Ollama `/api/generate` with `stream: true`
- Return response as ReadableStream (Server-Sent Events or chunked transfer)
- 60s timeout (longer than improve-prompt's 30s since full generation)
- Support AbortController for client-side cancellation
- Read ollamaUrl and model from AI settings store on client side

## Files
- Create: `src/app/api/playground/run/route.ts`
- Modify: `src/components/playground/PlaygroundPanel.tsx` - add run button, wire up API call
- Modify: `src/lib/playground-store.ts` - add `isRunning`, `currentResponse`, `abortController`, `systemPrompt`, `run()`, `stop()` actions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] POST `/api/playground/run` streams tokens from Ollama
- [ ] Client substitutes placeholder test values before sending
- [ ] Streaming response accumulates in playground store `currentResponse`
- [ ] AbortController cancels in-flight request on `stop()`
- [ ] Optional system prompt sent as separate Ollama system field
- [ ] Returns 400 for missing prompt, 502 for Ollama errors
- [ ] 60s timeout with clear error message
- [ ] `pnpm build` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] POST /api/playground/run accepts { prompt, model, ollamaUrl, systemPrompt? }
- [ ] Endpoint calls Ollama /api/generate with stream: true and returns ReadableStream
- [ ] Client substitutes test values into placeholder positions before sending prompt
- [ ] Playground store exposes run(snippetId, assembledPrompt) that calls endpoint and accumulates tokens in currentResponse
- [ ] store.stop() calls AbortController.abort() to cancel in-flight stream
- [ ] Optional systemPrompt sent as Ollama system field (separate from meta system prompt)
- [ ] Returns 400 for empty/missing prompt, 502 when Ollama unreachable
- [ ] 60s AbortSignal timeout on server side with clear error message

## Exit Condition
```bash
set -e
pnpm build
pnpm lint
npx tsc --noEmit
test -f src/app/api/playground/run/route.ts
grep -q "ReadableStream\|TransformStream" src/app/api/playground/run/route.ts
grep -q "stream.*true" src/app/api/playground/run/route.ts
grep -q "isRunning" src/lib/playground-store.ts
grep -q "abortController\|AbortController" src/lib/playground-store.ts
```

title: Epic: Snippet CRUD & Context Menu
description:
## Summary
Complete snippet CRUD operations in the UI. The store has deleteSnippet() but the UI has zero way to invoke it. Snippet context menu only has "Export Selected." Need delete, move-to-folder, rename, bulk operations, and keyboard shortcuts.

## Scope

- Bulk delete (multi-select)
- Full snippet context menu (delete, rename, move to folder, duplicate)

- Undo support for delete
- Confirmation dialog for destructive actions
- E2e tests for each story

## Quality Gates

```bash
npx playwright test --reporter=list
```

title: Snippet Includes (Composition)
description: Make {snippet name="..."} references actually resolve. Look up referenced snippet by title, inline its text, handle recursion/errors. Resolution in preview, playground, and export.

title: US-1: Snippet resolution engine
description:
As a developer, I need a pure function that resolves {snippet name="..."} references to actual snippet text so all downstream features (preview, playground, export) can use it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New `resolveSnippetIncludes(text, snippets)` function in `src/lib/raycast/snippet-resolver.ts`
- [ ] Looks up snippet by title (case-insensitive match)
- [ ] Returns resolved text with all `{snippet name="..."}` replaced by referenced content
- [ ] Recursive: if included snippet itself contains `{snippet ...}`, resolve those too
- [ ] Max recursion depth of 5; exceeding returns error marker `⚠ max depth: A → B → C → ...`
- [ ] Circular reference detection: `A → B → A` returns `⚠ circular: A → B → A`
- [ ] Applies modifiers (uppercase, lowercase, trim, percent-encode, json-stringify, raw) to resolved text
- [ ] Unresolved refs (no matching snippet) preserved with error metadata
- [ ] Function is pure (no side effects), takes snippets array as param
- [ ] Other placeholder types ({clipboard}, {argument}, etc.) pass through unmodified
- [ ] Returns both resolved text and error list for callers to inspect
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

## Exit Condition
```bash
set -e
# Structural: file exists with expected exports
test -f src/lib/raycast/snippet-resolver.ts
grep -q "resolveSnippetIncludes" src/lib/raycast/snippet-resolver.ts
grep -q "export" src/lib/raycast/snippet-resolver.ts

# Runtime: types + lint + build
npx tsc --noEmit
pnpm lint
pnpm build
```

title: US-2: Preview resolution + toggle
description:
As a user, I want the preview pane to show resolved snippet content inline so I can see the fully composed prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preview resolves `{snippet name="..."}` using resolveSnippetIncludes from snippet-resolver.ts
- [ ] Resolved text wrapped in visually distinct container (subtle left border + slight indent, muted background)
- [ ] Container shows small label like "→ greeting" to indicate source snippet
- [ ] Toggle button in preview header: "Resolve includes" on/off
- [ ] When off, shows existing placeholder pill (current behavior)
- [ ] Broken refs render as red pill with `⚠ not found: snippet-name`
- [ ] Circular/depth errors render as orange warning pill with the chain path
- [ ] Resolution updates reactively when a referenced snippet is edited
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] Verify in browser using playwright-cli skill: toggle works, resolved content displays, error states visible

## Exit Condition
```bash
set -e
# Structural: preview uses resolver + has toggle
grep -q "resolveSnippetIncludes" src/components/preview/Preview.tsx
grep -qE "resolve.*toggle|showResolved|resolveIncludes" src/components/preview/Preview.tsx

# Runtime: types + lint + build
```

title: US-1: Add aria labels, roles, and focus management
description:
## What
Add missing accessibility attributes across all interactive elements.

## Requirements
- Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons (toolbar, sidebar, modals)
- Add aria-expanded to folder chevron toggles in Sidebar.tsx
- Add role="menu" + role="menuitem" to context menus
- Add role="listbox" + role="option" + aria-selected to SearchPalette results
- Add aria-live="polite" region for toast notifications
- Focus trap on modals (FolderReorgModal, ImportModal, SettingsModal)
- Focus restore when modal/menu closes (return to trigger element)
- Add htmlFor linkage on EditorPanel keyword label
- Convert Sidebar delete dialog from custom div to <dialog> element

## Files
- src/app/page.tsx (toolbar buttons)
- src/components/Sidebar.tsx (folders, context menu, delete dialog)
- src/components/SearchPalette.tsx (results, keyboard nav)
- src/components/editor/EditorPanel.tsx (keyword label)
- src/components/FolderReorgModal.tsx (focus trap)
- src/components/ImportModal.tsx (focus trap)
- src/components/SettingsModal.tsx (focus trap)

## Exit Condition
```bash
set -e
# At least 5 aria-label attributes on toolbar buttons
ARIA_COUNT=$(grep -c 'aria-label' src/app/page.tsx)
test "$ARIA_COUNT" -ge 5
# Aria expanded on folder toggles
grep -q 'aria-expanded' src/components/Sidebar.tsx
# Context menu semantic roles
grep -q 'role="menu"' src/components/Sidebar.tsx
grep -q 'role="menuitem"' src/components/Sidebar.tsx
# Search palette roles
grep -q 'role="option"' src/components/SearchPalette.tsx
grep -q 'aria-selected' src/components/SearchPalette.tsx
# Focus trap on at least one modal
grep -qE 'focusTrap|focus-trap|onKeyDown.*Tab' src/components/FolderReorgModal.tsx
# Label linkage
grep -q 'htmlFor' src/components/editor/EditorPanel.tsx
npx tsc --noEmit
pnpm build
```

```bash
PID=$\!

STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/suggest-folder \
```

title: Resizable panels: replace fixed layout with react-resizable-panels
description:
## Summary
Replace fixed sidebar width + custom ResizableDivider with `react-resizable-panels` for all 3 panels (sidebar, editor, preview). Add sidebar collapse-to-icon-rail behavior.

## Current State
- **Sidebar**: fixed `w-64` (256px), no resize
- **Editor/Preview**: custom `ResizableDivider` component with mouse-only drag, percentage-based widths stored in localStorage (`dividerPercent`)
- **Preview toggle**: `previewVisible` state with ⌘\ shortcut
- Files: `page.tsx` (layout), `ResizableDivider.tsx` (divider), `Sidebar.tsx` (sidebar)

## Target State

### 1. Install react-resizable-panels
- `pnpm add react-resizable-panels`
- Replace flex layout in `page.tsx` with `PanelGroup > Panel + PanelResizeHandle` structure

### 2. Three-panel layout
```
[Sidebar | handle | Editor | handle | Preview]
```
- Sidebar: default 20%, min 15%, collapsible
- Editor: default 50%, min 20%
- Preview: default 30%, min 15%, collapsible (replaces current previewVisible toggle)

### 3. Sidebar collapse to icon rail
- Drag sidebar handle past threshold → collapses to ~40px icon rail
- Icon rail shows: expand chevron, new snippet (+), search/filter icon
- Click expand chevron or double-click handle to restore
- Use `Panel.collapse()` / `Panel.expand()` API

### 4. Preview panel collapse
- Replace current `previewVisible` boolean with panel collapse
- ⌘\ shortcut calls `panel.collapse()` / `panel.expand()` instead of toggling visibility
- Preserves previous width on re-expand

### 5. Persistence
- Use `autoSaveId` prop on `PanelGroup` for automatic localStorage persistence (replaces manual `dividerPercent` persistence)
- Remove `leftPercent` state and `dividerPercent` localStorage key

### 6. Cleanup
- Delete `ResizableDivider.tsx`
- Remove `leftPercent`, `handleResize`, `dividerPercent` persistence from `page.tsx`
- Remove `ResizableDivider` import

## Files to modify
- `src/app/page.tsx` - replace layout, remove old resize state
- `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` - add collapsed icon rail variant
- `src/components/ResizableDivider.tsx` - DELETE

## Files to create
- `src/components/SidebarRail.tsx` - collapsed icon rail component (expand, new snippet, filter icons)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 3 panels resizable via drag handles
- [ ] Sidebar collapses to icon rail with expand/new/filter icons
- [ ] Preview collapses via drag or ⌘\ shortcut
- [ ] Panel sizes persist across sessions via autoSaveId
- [ ] Double-click handle resets to default sizes
- [ ] Keyboard accessible (arrow keys on focused handle)
- [ ] No layout shift on page load (SSR-safe)
- [ ] `pnpm build` passes
- [ ] Existing e2e tests pass (sidebar testids preserved)

## Technical Notes
- react-resizable-panels handles touch, keyboard, a11y out of the box
- `PanelResizeHandle` supports custom styling (match current accent border look)
- `onCollapse`/`onExpand` callbacks for sidebar rail toggle
- `autoSaveId="prompt-workbench-layout"` handles all persistence

### Playwright Test Requirements

The Playwright spec MUST verify:
1. **Folder renders with chevron** — `page.locator('[data-testid="folder-row"]')` visible with chevron icon
2. **Click chevron expands/collapses** — child snippets toggle visibility on click
3. **Nested indent** — subfolder has greater left padding than parent
4. **Snippet count** — folder row shows `(N)` count matching actual snippets inside
5. **Unfiled snippets** — snippets without folderId render at root level, not inside any folder
6. **Empty state** — folder with 0 snippets shows no count badge

```gherkin
Feature: Folder Data Model & Tree Display

  Background:
```
